    16→module
    17→
    18→public import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Acyclic
    19→public import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Bipartite
    20→public import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Matching
    21→public import Mathlib.Data.Real.Archimedean
    22→public import Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.PiL2
    23→
    24→@[expose] public section
    25→
    26→namespace SimpleGraph
    27→
    28→open Classical Finset List
    29→
    30→variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α]
    31→
    32→/-- `Ls G` is the maximum number of leaves over all spanning trees of `G`.
    33→It is defined to be `0` when `G` is not connected. -/
    34→noncomputable def Ls (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℝ :=
    35→  letI spanningTrees := { T : Subgraph G | T.IsSpanning ∧ IsTree T.coe }
    36→  letI leaves (T : Subgraph G) := T.verts.toFinset.filter (fun v => T.degree v = 1)
    37→  letI num_leaves (T : Subgraph G) := (leaves T).card
    38→  sSup (Set.image (fun T => (num_leaves T : ℝ)) spanningTrees)
    39→
    40→/-- `n G` is the number of vertices of `G` as a real number. -/
    41→noncomputable def n (_ : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ := Fintype.card α
    42→
    43→/-- `m G` is the size of a maximum matching of `G`. -/
    44→noncomputable def m (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℝ :=
    45→  let matchings := { M : Subgraph G | M.IsMatching }
    46→  sSup (Set.image (fun M => (M.edgeSet.toFinset.card : ℝ)) matchings)
    47→
    48→/-- The maximum cardinality among all independent sets `s`
    49→    that maximize the quantity `|s| - |N(s)|`, where `N(s)`
    50→    is the neighborhood of the set `s`. -/
    51→noncomputable def aprime (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℝ :=
    52→  letI indep_sets : Finset (Finset α) := univ.powerset.filter (fun s => G.IsIndepSet (s : Set α))
    53→  letI diff (s : Finset α) : ℤ := (s.card : ℤ) - (⋃ v ∈ (s : Set α), G.neighborSet v).toFinset.card
    54→  letI max_diff := (indep_sets.image diff).max
    55→  letI critical_sets := indep_sets.filter (fun s ↦ diff s = max_diff.getD 0)
    56→  letI max_card := (critical_sets.image Finset.card).max
    57→  (max_card.getD 0 : ℝ)
    58→
    59→/-- `largestInducedForestSize G` is the size of a largest induced forest of `G`. -/
    60→noncomputable def largestInducedForestSize (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ :=
    61→  sSup { n | ∃ s : Finset α, (G.induce s).IsAcyclic ∧ s.card = n }
    62→
    63→/-- The degree sequence of a graph, sorted in nondecreasing order. -/
    64→noncomputable def degreeSequence (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : List ℕ :=
    65→  (Finset.univ.val.map fun v : α => G.degree v).sort (· ≤ ·)
    66→
    67→/--
    68→The maximum number of occurrences of any term of the degree sequence of `G`.
    69→-/
    70→noncomputable def degreeSequenceMultiplicity (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℕ :=
    71→  letI degs := degreeSequence G
    72→  (List.max? (degs.map (fun d => degs.count d))).getD 0
    73→
    74→/-- `largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize G` is the size of a largest induced
    75→bipartite subgraph of `G`. -/
    76→noncomputable def largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ :=
    77→  sSup { n | ∃ s : Finset α, (G.induce s).IsBipartite ∧ s.card = n }
    78→
    79→/-- `b G` is the number of vertices of a largest induced bipartite subgraph of `G`.
    80→Returned as a real number. -/
    81→noncomputable def b (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ :=
    82→  (largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize G : ℝ)
    83→
    84→/-- Independence number of the neighbourhood of `v`. -/
    85→noncomputable def indepNeighborsCard (G : SimpleGraph α) (v : α) : ℕ :=
    86→  (G.induce (G.neighborSet v)).indepNum
    87→
    88→/-- The same quantity as a real number. -/
    89→noncomputable def indepNeighbors (G : SimpleGraph α) (v : α) : ℝ :=
    90→  (indepNeighborsCard G v : ℝ)
    91→
    92→/-- Average of `indepNeighbors` over all vertices. -/
    93→noncomputable def averageIndepNeighbors (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ :=
    94→  (∑ v ∈ Finset.univ, indepNeighbors G v) / (Fintype.card α : ℝ)
    95→
    96→/-- A unit distance graph in ℝ²:
    97→A graph where the vertices V are a collection of points in ℝ² and there is
    98→an edge between two points if and only if the distance between them is 1. -/
    99→def UnitDistancePlaneGraph (V : Set (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 2))) : SimpleGraph V where
   100→  Adj x y := Dist.dist x y = 1
   101→  symm x y := by simp [PseudoMetricSpace.dist_comm]
   102→
   103→/--
   104→Two walks are internally disjoint if they share no vertices other than their endpoints.
   105→-/
   106→def InternallyDisjoint {V : Type*} {G : SimpleGraph V} {u v x y : V}
   107→    (p : G.Walk u v) (q : G.Walk x y) : Prop :=
   108→  Disjoint p.support.tail.dropLast q.support.tail.dropLast
   109→
   110→/--
   111→We say a graph is infinitely connected if any two vertices are connected by infinitely many
   112→pairwise disjoint paths.
   113→-/
   114→def InfinitelyConnected {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Prop :=
   115→  Pairwise fun u v ↦ ∃ P : Set (G.Walk u v),
   116→    P.Infinite ∧ (∀ p ∈ P, p.IsPath) ∧ P.Pairwise InternallyDisjoint
   117→
   118→/-- Infinite graphs: definitions for max degree and clique number so that the maximum
   119→degree (resp. clique number) of a graph with unbounded degree (resp. clique size) is
   120→`∞` rather than 0.
   121→-/
   122→noncomputable
   123→def edegree {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (v : V) : ℕ∞ := (G.neighborSet v).encard
   124→
   125→noncomputable
   126→def emaxDegree {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ℕ∞ := ⨆ v, G.edegree v
   127→
   128→noncomputable
   129→def ecliqueNum {V : Type} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ℕ∞ := ⨆ (s : Finset V) (_ : G.IsClique s), #s
   130→
   131→end SimpleGraph
   132→

    16→module
    17→
    18→public import FormalConjecturesForMathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.GraphConjectures.Definitions
    19→public import FormalConjecturesForMathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.GraphConjectures.Domination
    20→public import Mathlib.Analysis.Matrix.Spectrum
    21→public import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.AdjMatrix
    22→public import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Metric
    23→public import Mathlib.Data.Multiset.Interval
    24→
    25→@[expose] public section
    26→
    27→noncomputable def Matrix.IsHermitian.maxEigenvalue {𝕜 : Type*} [Field 𝕜] [RCLike 𝕜]
    28→    {n : Type*} [Fintype n] [DecidableEq n] {A : Matrix n n 𝕜} (hA : A.IsHermitian) : ℝ :=
    29→  iSup hA.eigenvalues
    30→
    31→namespace SimpleGraph
    32→
    33→open Classical
    34→
    35→variable {α : Type*} [Fintype α] [DecidableEq α]
    36→
    37→/-- Abbreviation for the average independence number of the neighborhoods.
    38→-/
    39→noncomputable abbrev l (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ := averageIndepNeighbors G
    40→
    41→/-- The same quantity under a different name, used in some conjectures.
    42→-/
    43→noncomputable abbrev l_avg (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ := averageIndepNeighbors G
    44→
    45→/-- Independent domination number of `G`. -/
    46→noncomputable def gi (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ := G.indepDominationNumber
    47→
    48→/-- `temp_v G v = deg(v)/(n(G) - deg(v))`. -/
    49→noncomputable def temp_v (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] (v : α) : ℝ :=
    50→  let n := Fintype.card α
    51→  let deg := G.degree v
    52→  if n = deg then 0 else (deg : ℝ) / ((n : ℝ) - (deg : ℝ))
    53→
    54→/-- Maximum of `temp_v` over all vertices. -/
    55→noncomputable def MaxTemp (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] [Fintype α] [Nonempty α] : ℝ :=
    56→  let temps := Finset.univ.image (temp_v G)
    57→  temps.max' (Finset.image_nonempty.mpr Finset.univ_nonempty)
    58→
    59→/-- Cardinality of the union of the neighbourhoods of the ends of the non-edge `e`. -/
    60→def non_edge_neighborhood_card (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] (e : Sym2 α) : ℕ :=
    61→  Sym2.lift ⟨fun u v => (G.neighborFinset u ∪ G.neighborFinset v).card,
    62→    fun u v => by simp [Finset.union_comm]⟩ e
    63→
    64→/-- Minimum size of the neighbourhood of a non-edge of `G`. -/
    65→noncomputable def NG (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℝ :=
    66→  let non_edges := (compl G).edgeFinset
    67→  if h : non_edges.Nonempty then
    68→    let neighbor_sizes := non_edges.image (non_edge_neighborhood_card G)
    69→    (neighbor_sizes.min' (Finset.Nonempty.image h _))
    70→  else
    71→    (Fintype.card α : ℝ)
    72→
    73→/-- Size of a largest induced forest of `G` expressed as a real number. -/
    74→noncomputable def S (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ :=
    75→  let card := Fintype.card α
    76→  if card < 2 then 0 else
    77→    let degrees := Multiset.ofList (List.map (fun v => G.degree v) Finset.univ.toList)
    78→    let sorted_degrees := degrees.sort (· ≤ ·)
    79→    ↑((sorted_degrees[card - 2]?).getD 0)
    80→
    81→/-- Local eccentricity of a vertex. -/
    82→noncomputable def local_eccentricity (G : SimpleGraph α) (v : α) : ENat :=
    83→  sSup (Set.range (G.edist v))
    84→
    85→/-- The largest integer less than or equal to `x`. -/
    86→noncomputable def FLOOR (x : ℝ) : ℝ := ⌊x⌋
    87→
    88→/-- Eccentricity of a vertex. -/
    89→noncomputable def eccentricity (G : SimpleGraph α) (v : α) : ℕ∞ :=
    90→  sSup (Set.range (G.edist v))
    91→
    92→/-- The minimum eccentricity among all vertices. -/
    93→noncomputable def minEccentricity (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ∞ :=
    94→  sInf (Set.range (eccentricity G))
    95→
    96→/-- The set of vertices of minimum eccentricity. -/
    97→noncomputable def graphCenter (G : SimpleGraph α) : Set α :=
    98→  {v : α | eccentricity G v = minEccentricity G}
    99→
   100→/-- The maximum eccentricity among all vertices. -/
   101→noncomputable def maxEccentricity (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ∞ :=
   102→  sSup (Set.range (eccentricity G))
   103→
   104→/-- The set of vertices of maximum eccentricity. -/
   105→noncomputable def maxEccentricityVertices (G : SimpleGraph α) : Set α :=
   106→  {v : α | eccentricity G v = maxEccentricity G}
   107→
   108→/-- Distance from a vertex to a finite set. -/
   109→noncomputable def distToSet (G : SimpleGraph α) (v : α) (S : Set α) : ℕ :=
   110→  if h : S.toFinset.Nonempty then
   111→    (S.toFinset.image (fun s => G.dist v s)).min' (Finset.Nonempty.image h _)
   112→  else 0
   113→
   114→/-- Average distance of `G`. -/
   115→noncomputable def averageDistance (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ :=
   116→  if Fintype.card α > 1 then
   117→    (∑ u ∈ Finset.univ, ∑ v ∈ Finset.univ, (G.dist u v : ℝ)) /
   118→      ((Fintype.card α : ℝ) * ((Fintype.card α : ℝ) - 1))
   119→  else
   120→    0
   121→
   122→/-- The floor of the average distance of `G`. -/
   123→noncomputable def path (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ :=
   124→  if G.Connected then
   125→    Nat.floor (averageDistance G)
   126→  else
   127→    0
   128→
   129→/-- Auxiliary quantity `ecc` used in conjecture 34. -/
   130→noncomputable def ecc (G : SimpleGraph α) (S : Set α) : ℕ :=
   131→  let s_comp := Finset.univ.filter (fun v => v ∉ S)
   132→  if h : s_comp.Nonempty then
   133→    (s_comp.image (fun v => distToSet G v S)).max' (Finset.Nonempty.image h _)
   134→  else 0
   135→
   136→/-- Average distance from all vertices to a given set. -/
   137→noncomputable def distavg (G : SimpleGraph α) (S : Set α) : ℝ :=
   138→  if Fintype.card α > 0 then
   139→    (∑ v ∈ Finset.univ, (distToSet G v S : ℝ)) / (Fintype.card α : ℝ)
   140→  else
   141→    0
   142→
   143→/-- A family of paths covering all vertices without overlaps. -/
   144→def IsPathCover (G : SimpleGraph α) (P : Finset (Finset α)) : Prop :=
   145→  (∀ s1 ∈ P, ∀ s2 ∈ P, s1 ≠ s2 → Disjoint s1 s2) ∧
   146→  (Finset.univ ⊆ P.biUnion id) ∧
   147→  (∀ s ∈ P, ∃ (u v : α) (p : G.Walk u v), p.IsPath ∧ s = p.support.toFinset)
   148→
   149→/-- Minimum size of a path cover of `G`. -/
   150→noncomputable def pathCoverNumber (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ :=
   151→  sInf { k | ∃ P : Finset (Finset α), P.card = k ∧ IsPathCover G P }
   152→
   153→/-- The same quantity as a real number. -/
   154→noncomputable def p (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℝ := (pathCoverNumber G : ℝ)
   155→
   156→/-- The Wiener index of `G`, which is the sum of distances between all
   157→pairs of vertices. -/
   158→noncomputable def wienerIndex (G : SimpleGraph α) : ℕ :=
   159→  ∑ uv : Sym2 α, uv.lift ⟨fun u v ↦ G.dist u v, by simp [dist_comm]⟩
   160→
   161→/-- Auxiliary function for Szeged index: counts vertices closer to u than v. -/
   162→noncomputable def szeged_aux (G : SimpleGraph α) (u v : α) : ℕ :=
   163→  (Finset.univ.filter (fun w => G.edist w u < G.edist w v)).card
   164→
   165→/-- The Szeged index of `G`.
   166→
   167→This is define as the sum `∑_{uv ∈ E(G)} n_u(u,v) * n_v(u,v)` where
   168→`n_u(uv)` is the number of vertices closer to `u` than `v`.
   169→-/
   170→noncomputable def szegedIndex (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℕ :=
   171→  ∑ e ∈ G.edgeFinset,
   172→    e.lift ⟨fun u v => szeged_aux G u v * szeged_aux G v u, by simp [mul_comm]⟩
   173→
   174→/-- The average degree of `G`. -/
   175→noncomputable def averageDegree (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℚ  :=
   176→  (∑ v, (G.degree v : ℚ)) / (Fintype.card α : ℚ)
   177→
   178→/-- The multiset of degrees of a graph. -/
   179→def degreeMultiset (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : Multiset ℕ :=
   180→  Finset.univ.val.map fun v => G.degree v
   181→
   182→/-- The annihilation number of a graph. This is the largest number of degrees that can be added
   183→together without going over the total number of edges of that graph. -/
   184→def annihilationNumber (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℕ :=
   185→  -- Calculate the limit: The number of edges (Sum of degrees / 2)
   186→  letI limit := G.edgeFinset.card
   187→
   188→  -- The set of all multisets of degrees that sum to less than or equal to `limit`
   189→  Finset.Iic (degreeMultiset G)
   190→    |>.filter (fun S ↦ Multiset.sum S ≤ limit)
   191→    |>.sup Multiset.card
   192→
   193→/--
   194→Computes the annihilation number of a graph G.
   195→
   196→It calculates the degree sequence, sorts it ascendingly, and finds the largest prefix length 'k'
   197→(where `0 ≤ k ≤ |V(G)|`) such that the sum of the prefix is less than or equal to the sum of the corresponding suffix.
   198→-/
   199→noncomputable def annihilationNumber' (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℕ :=
   200→  -- 1. Get the degree sequence sorted in ascending order.
   201→  -- G.degree_list returns the list of degrees.
   202→  letI degrees := (Finset.univ.image fun v => G.degree v).sort (· ≤ ·)
   203→
   204→  -- 2. Define the condition for the annihilation number.
   205→  -- k represents the number of smallest degrees considered (the length of the prefix).
   206→  letI condition (k : ℕ) : Bool := (degrees.take k).sum ≤ (degrees.drop k).sum
   207→
   208→  -- 3. Find the maximum k in {0, ..., n} satisfying the condition.
   209→  -- List.range (n + 1) generates the list [0, 1, ..., n].
   210→  letI candidates := (List.range (Fintype.card α + 1)).filter condition
   211→
   212→  -- 4. Return the maximum candidate.
   213→  -- The list of candidates is guaranteed to be non-empty because k=0 always satisfies
   214→  -- the condition (0 ≤ sum of all degrees).
   215→  candidates.getLast!
   216→
   217→set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false in
   218→-- TODO(Paul-Lez): debug the issue with the unused variable linter...
   219→proof_wanted annihilationNumberEq (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] :
   220→    annihilationNumber G = annihilationNumber' G
   221→
   222→/-!
   223→## Residue
   224→The residue of a graph is the number of zeros remaining after iteratively applying the Havel-Hakimi
   225→algorithm to the degree sequence until all remaining degrees are zero.
   226→-/
   227→
   228→/--
   229→Helper function: Performs one step of the Havel-Hakimi reduction on a degree sequence.
   230→Assumes the input list `s` is sorted descending.
   231→Removes the first element `d`, decrements the next `d` elements by 1, and re-sorts the list descending.
   232→
   233→Note: when `s` is the list of vertices arising from a simple graph, if the first index is `s` then
   234→the degree list always has length at least `s+1` so this makes sense.
   235→-/
   236→def havelHakimiStep (s : List ℕ) : List ℕ :=
   237→  match s with
   238→  | [] => []
   239→  | d :: rest =>
   240→    -- Split the rest into the part to decrement (first d elements) and the remaining part.
   241→    let (to_decrement, remaining) := rest.splitAt d
   242→    -- Decrement the elements
   243→    let decremented := to_decrement.map (· - 1)
   244→    -- Combine and re-sort descending.
   245→    (decremented ++ remaining).mergeSort (· ≥ ·)
   246→
   247→/--
   248→Auxiliary function to calculate the residue recursively.
   249→Applies Havel-Hakimi steps until the sequence consists only of zeros or is empty.
   250→-/
   251→partial def residueAux : List ℕ → ℕ
   252→  | [] => 0        -- Empty sequence, residue is 0.
   253→  | 0 :: s => 1 + s.length -- If the largest degree is 0 (and the list is sorted), all are 0.
   254→  | s => residueAux (havelHakimiStep s) -- Apply one reduction step and recurse.
   255→
   256→/--
   257→Computes the residue of a graph G, ,i.e. the number of zeros remaining after iteratively applying the Havel-Hakimi
   258→algorithm to the degree sequence until all remaining degrees are zero.
   259→Starts with the descending degree sequence and applies the Havel-Hakimi process.
   260→-/
   261→noncomputable def residue (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℕ :=
   262→  -- Get the degree sequence sorted in descending order and apply `residueAux`.
   263→  residueAux ((Finset.univ.image fun v => G.degree v).sort (· ≥ ·))
   264→
   265→/--
   266→Fractional alpha. This is defined as
   267→$$
   268→\max_x \sum_{v \in V} x_v
   269→$$
   270→subject to $0 \le x_v \le 1$ for all $v \in V$ and
   271→$x_u + x_v \le 1$ whenever $(u, v)$ are connected by an edge.
   272→-/
   273→noncomputable def fractionalAlpha (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℝ :=
   274→  sSup {(∑ i, x i) | (x : α → ℝ) (_ : ∀ v, x v ∈ Set.Icc 0 1)
   275→    (_ : ∀ u v, G.Adj u v → x u + x v ≤ 1)}
   276→
   277→/--
   278→Lovász Theta Function (ϑ(G))
   279→The Lovász theta function is defined as:
   280→ϑ(G) = min λ_max(A)
   281→where the minimum is taken over all symmetric matrices A such that:
   282→
   283→A_ij = 1 for all i = j (diagonal entries are 1)
   284→A_ij = 0 for all {i,j} ∈ E (entries corresponding to edges are 0)
   285→A is positive semidefinite
   286→
   287→Here λ_max(A) denotes the maximum eigenvalue of A.
   288→-/
   289→noncomputable def lovaczThetaFunction
   290→    (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℝ :=
   291→  sInf {(Matrix.IsHermitian.maxEigenvalue hA) | (A : Matrix α α ℝ) (hA : A.IsHermitian)
   292→      (_ : ∀ i, A i i = 1) (_ : ∀ i j, G.Adj i j → A i j = 0)}
   293→
   294→/--
   295→Given a simple graph `G` with adjacency matrix `A`, this is the number `n₀ + min n₊ n₋` where:
   296→- `n₀` is the multiplicity of zero as an eigenvalue of `A`
   297→- `n₊` is the number of positive eigenvalues of `A` (counting multiplicities)
   298→- `n₋` is the number of negative eigenvalues of `A` (counting multiplicities)
   299→-/
   300→noncomputable def cvetkovic (G : SimpleGraph α) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ℕ :=
   301→  letI A : Matrix α α ℝ := G.adjMatrix ℝ
   302→  letI spectrum : Multiset ℝ := (Matrix.charpoly A).roots
   303→  letI positive_count : ℕ := spectrum.countP (fun x => 0 < x)
   304→  letI negative_count : ℕ := spectrum.countP (fun x => 0 > x)
   305→  letI zero_count : ℕ := spectrum.countP (fun x => 0 = x)
   306→  zero_count + min positive_count negative_count
   307→
   308→end SimpleGraph
   309→

1→/-
     2→Copyright (c) 2025 Mitchell Horner. All rights reserved.
     3→Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
     4→Authors: Mitchell Horner
     5→-/
     6→module
     7→
     8→public import Mathlib.Algebra.Order.Floor.Semiring
     9→public import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Copy
    10→
    11→/-!
    12→# Extremal graph theory
    13→
    14→This file introduces basic definitions for extremal graph theory, including extremal numbers.
    15→
    16→## Main definitions
    17→
    18→* `SimpleGraph.IsExtremal` is the predicate that `G` has the maximum number of edges of any simple
    19→  graph, with fixed vertices, satisfying `p`.
    20→
    21→* `SimpleGraph.extremalNumber` is the maximum number of edges in a `H`-free simple graph on `n`
    22→  vertices.
    23→
    24→  If `H` is contained in all simple graphs on `n` vertices, then this is `0`.
    25→-/
    26→
    27→@[expose] public section
    28→
    29→assert_not_exists Field
    30→
    31→open Finset Fintype
    32→
    33→namespace SimpleGraph
    34→
    35→section IsExtremal
    36→
    37→variable {V : Type*} [Fintype V] {G : SimpleGraph V} [DecidableRel G.Adj]
    38→
    39→/-- `G` is an extremal graph satisfying `p` if `G` has the maximum number of edges of any simple
    40→graph, with fixed vertices, satisfying `p`. -/
    41→def IsExtremal (G : SimpleGraph V) [DecidableRel G.Adj] (p : SimpleGraph V → Prop) :=
    42→  p G ∧ ∀ ⦃G' : SimpleGraph V⦄ [DecidableRel G'.Adj], p G' → #G'.edgeFinset ≤ #G.edgeFinset
    43→
    44→lemma IsExtremal.prop {p : SimpleGraph V → Prop} (h : G.IsExtremal p) : p G := h.1
    45→
    46→open Classical in
    47→/-- If one simple graph satisfies `p`, then there exists an extremal graph satisfying `p`. -/
    48→theorem exists_isExtremal_iff_exists (p : SimpleGraph V → Prop) :
    49→    (∃ G : SimpleGraph V, ∃ _ : DecidableRel G.Adj, G.IsExtremal p) ↔ ∃ G, p G := by
    50→  refine ⟨fun ⟨_, _, h⟩ ↦ ⟨_, h.1⟩, fun ⟨G, hp⟩ ↦ ?_⟩
    51→  obtain ⟨G', hp', h⟩ := by
    52→    apply exists_max_image { G | p G } (#·.edgeFinset)
    53→    use G, by simpa using hp
    54→  use G', inferInstanceAs (DecidableRel G'.Adj)
    55→  exact ⟨by simpa using hp', fun _ _ hp ↦ by convert h _ (by simpa using hp)⟩
    56→
    57→/-- If `H` has at least one edge, then there exists an extremal `H.Free` graph. -/
    58→theorem exists_isExtremal_free {W : Type*} {H : SimpleGraph W} (h : H ≠ ⊥) :
    59→    ∃ G : SimpleGraph V, ∃ _ : DecidableRel G.Adj, G.IsExtremal H.Free :=
    60→  (exists_isExtremal_iff_exists H.Free).mpr ⟨⊥, free_bot h⟩
    61→
    62→open Classical in
    63→theorem IsExtremal.le_iff_eq
    64→    {p : SimpleGraph V → Prop} (hG : G.IsExtremal p) {H : SimpleGraph V} (hH : p H) :
    65→    G ≤ H ↔ G = H :=
    66→  ⟨fun hGH ↦ edgeFinset_inj.1 <|
    67→    eq_of_subset_of_card_le (edgeFinset_subset_edgeFinset.2 hGH) (hG.2 hH), le_of_eq⟩
    68→
    69→end IsExtremal
    70→
    71→section ExtremalNumber
    72→
    73→open Classical in
    74→/-- The extremal number of a natural number `n` and a simple graph `H` is the maximum number of
    75→edges in a `H`-free simple graph on `n` vertices.
    76→
    77→If `H` is contained in all simple graphs on `n` vertices, then this is `0`. -/
    78→noncomputable def extremalNumber (n : ℕ) {W : Type*} (H : SimpleGraph W) : ℕ :=
    79→  sup { G : SimpleGraph (Fin n) | H.Free G } (#·.edgeFinset)
    80→
    81→variable {n : ℕ} {V W : Type*} {G : SimpleGraph V} {H : SimpleGraph W}
    82→
    83→open Classical in
    84→theorem extremalNumber_of_fintypeCard_eq [Fintype V] (hc : card V = n) :
    85→    extremalNumber n H = sup { G : SimpleGraph V | H.Free G } (#·.edgeFinset) := by
    86→  let e := Fintype.equivFinOfCardEq hc
    87→  rw [extremalNumber, le_antisymm_iff]
    88→  and_intros
    89→  on_goal 1 =>
    90→    replace e := e.symm
    91→  all_goals
    92→  rw [Finset.sup_le_iff]
    93→  intro G h
    94→  let G' := G.map e.toEmbedding
    95→  replace h' : G' ∈ univ.filter (H.Free ·) := by
    96→    rw [mem_filter, ← free_congr .refl (.map e G)]
    97→    simpa using h
    98→  rw [Iso.card_edgeFinset_eq (.map e G)]
    99→  convert @le_sup _ _ _ _ { G | H.Free G } (#·.edgeFinset) G' h'
   100→

    16→
    17→import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18→
    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 765
    21→
    22→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/765](https://www.erdosproblems.com/765)
    23→
    24→Give an asymptotic formula for $\mathrm{ex}(n; C_4)$.
    25→
    26→Erdős and Klein proved $\mathrm{ex}(n; C_4) \asymp n^{3/2}$. Reiman proved
    27→$\frac{1}{2\sqrt{2}} \leq \lim \frac{\mathrm{ex}(n; C_4)}{n^{3/2}} \leq \frac{1}{2}$.
    28→Erdős–Rényi and independently Brown showed that for $n = q^2 + q + 1$ with $q$ a
    29→prime power, $\mathrm{ex}(n; C_4) \geq \frac{q(q+1)^2}{2}$, which together with Reiman's
    30→upper bound gives $\mathrm{ex}(n; C_4) \sim \frac{1}{2} n^{3/2}$.
    31→
    32→[Er93] Erdős, P., *On some of my favourite theorems*. Combinatorics, Paul Erdős is eighty,
    33→Vol. 2 (Keszthely, 1993), 97–132.
    34→
    35→[MaYa23] Ma, J. and Yang, T., *On the extremal number of subdivisions*. Combinatorica 43
    36→(2023), 1019–1027.
    37→-/
    38→
    39→open SimpleGraph
    40→
    41→namespace Erdos765
    42→
    43→/-- The cycle graph $C_m$ on $m$ vertices ($m \geq 3$). -/
    44→def cycleGraph (m : ℕ) (_ : m ≥ 3) : SimpleGraph (Fin m) where
    45→  Adj i j := i ≠ j ∧ (j.val = (i.val + 1) % m ∨ i.val = (j.val + 1) % m)
    46→  symm := fun _ _ ⟨hne, h⟩ => ⟨hne.symm, h.elim Or.inr Or.inl⟩
    47→  loopless := fun _ ⟨h, _⟩ => h rfl
    48→
    49→/-- A graph $G$ contains $H$ as a subgraph via an injective graph homomorphism. -/
    50→def ContainsSubgraph {V U : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (H : SimpleGraph U) : Prop :=
    51→  ∃ f : U → V, Function.Injective f ∧ ∀ u v : U, H.Adj u v → G.Adj (f u) (f v)
    52→
    53→/-- The Turán extremal number $\mathrm{ex}(n; H)$: the maximum number of edges in a
    54→simple graph on $n$ vertices that does not contain $H$ as a subgraph. -/
    55→noncomputable def extremalNumber {U : Type*} (H : SimpleGraph U) (n : ℕ) : ℕ :=
    56→  sSup {m : ℕ | ∃ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
    57→    ¬ContainsSubgraph G H ∧ G.edgeSet.ncard = m}
    58→
    59→/--
    60→Erdős Problem 765 (SOLVED):
    61→
    62→The asymptotic formula for $\mathrm{ex}(n; C_4)$ is
    63→$\mathrm{ex}(n; C_4) \sim \frac{1}{2} n^{3/2}$.
    64→
    65→Formally: for every $\varepsilon > 0$, there exists $N_0$ such that for all $n \geq N_0$,
    66→$$\left|\frac{\mathrm{ex}(n; C_4)}{n^{3/2}} - \frac{1}{2}\right| < \varepsilon.$$
    67→-/
    68→@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    69→theorem erdos_765 :
    70→    ∀ ε : ℝ, ε > 0 →
    71→    ∃ N₀ : ℕ, ∀ n : ℕ, N₀ ≤ n →
    72→      |(↑(extremalNumber (cycleGraph 4 (by omega)) n) / (n : ℝ) ^ ((3 : ℝ) / 2) - 1 / 2)| < ε := by
    73→  sorry
    74→
    75→/--
    76→Erdős's refined conjecture from [Er93] (still open):
    77→
    78→$\mathrm{ex}(n; C_4) = \frac{n^{3/2}}{2} + O(n)$.
    79→
    80→Formally: there exists a constant $C > 0$ such that for all $n \geq 1$,
    81→$$\left|\mathrm{ex}(n; C_4) - \frac{n^{3/2}}{2}\right| \leq C \cdot n.$$
    82→
    83→His even stronger conjecture with $n/4$ second-order term was disproved by Ma–Yang [MaYa23].
    84→-/
    85→@[category research open, AMS 5]
    86→theorem erdos_765.variants.refined :
    87→    ∃ C : ℝ, 0 < C ∧
    88→    ∀ n : ℕ, 1 ≤ n →
    89→      |(↑(extremalNumber (cycleGraph 4 (by omega)) n) - (n : ℝ) ^ ((3 : ℝ) / 2) / 2)| ≤
    90→        C * (n : ℝ) := by
    91→  sorry
    92→
    93→end Erdos765
    94→

    22→Is it true that for every rational α ∈ [1, 2), there exists a bipartite graph G such that
    23→the Turán extremal number satisfies ex(n; G) ≍ n^α?
    24→
    25→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/571](https://www.erdosproblems.com/571)
    26→
    27→[Er78] Erdős, P., _Problems and results in combinatorial analysis and combinatorial
    28→  number theory_, Proceedings of the Ninth Southeastern Conference on Combinatorics,
    29→  Graph Theory, and Computing (1978), 29–40.
    30→
    31→[Er81] Erdős, P., _On the combinatorial problems which I would most like to see solved_,
    32→  Combinatorica **1** (1981), 25–42.

    34→[ErSi84] Erdős, P. and Simonovits, M., _Cube-supersaturated graphs and related problems_,
    35→  Progress in graph theory (Waterloo, Ont., 1982), Academic Press, Toronto, ON, 1984, 203–218.
    36→
    37→[BuCo18] Bukh, B. and Conlon, D., _Rational exponents in extremal graph theory_,
    38→  Journal of the European Mathematical Society (JEMS) (2018), 1747–1757.

    43→namespace Erdos571

    47→  ∃ f : U → V, Function.Injective f ∧ ∀ u v : U, H.Adj u v → G.Adj (f u) (f v)
    48→
    49→/-- The Turán extremal number $\mathrm{ex}(n; H)$: the maximum number of edges in a
    50→simple graph on $n$ vertices that does not contain $H$ as a subgraph. -/
    51→noncomputable def extremalNumber {U : Type*} (H : SimpleGraph U) (n : ℕ) : ℕ :=
    52→  sSup {m : ℕ | ∃ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
    53→    ¬ContainsSubgraph G H ∧ G.edgeSet.ncard = m}
    54→
    55→/--
    56→Erdős Problem 571 (Erdős–Simonovits):
    57→
    58→For every rational $\alpha \in [1, 2)$, there exists a finite bipartite graph $G$ such that
    59→the Turán extremal number satisfies $\mathrm{ex}(n; G) \asymp n^\alpha$, meaning there exist positive
    60→constants $c_1, c_2 > 0$ and a threshold $N_0$ such that for all $n \geq N_0$,
    61→$$c_1 \cdot n^\alpha \leq \mathrm{ex}(n; G) \leq c_2 \cdot n^\alpha.$$
    62→-/
    63→@[category research open, AMS 5]
    64→theorem erdos_571 :
    65→    ∀ α : ℚ, 1 ≤ α → α < 2 →
    66→    ∃ (U : Type) (_ : Fintype U) (G : SimpleGraph U),
    67→      G.Colorable 2 ∧
    68→      ∃ c₁ c₂ : ℝ, 0 < c₁ ∧ 0 < c₂ ∧
    69→      ∃ N₀ : ℕ, ∀ n : ℕ, N₀ ≤ n →
    70→        c₁ * (n : ℝ) ^ (α : ℝ) ≤ (extremalNumber G n : ℝ) ∧
    71→        (extremalNumber G n : ℝ) ≤ c₂ * (n : ℝ) ^ (α : ℝ) := by
    72→  sorry
    73→
    74→end Erdos571

1→/-
     2→Copyright (c) 2025 Mitchell Horner. All rights reserved.
     3→Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
     4→Authors: Mitchell Horner
     5→-/
     6→module
     7→
     8→public import Mathlib.Algebra.Order.Floor.Semiring
     9→public import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Copy
    10→
    11→/-!
    12→# Extremal graph theory
    13→
    14→This file introduces basic definitions for extremal graph theory, including extremal numbers.
    15→
    16→## Main definitions
    17→
    18→* `SimpleGraph.IsExtremal` is the predicate that `G` has the maximum number of edges of any simple
    19→  graph, with fixed vertices, satisfying `p`.
    20→
    21→* `SimpleGraph.extremalNumber` is the maximum number of edges in a `H`-free simple graph on `n`
    22→  vertices.
    23→
    24→  If `H` is contained in all simple graphs on `n` vertices, then this is `0`.
    25→-/
    26→
    27→@[expose] public section
    28→
    29→assert_not_exists Field
    30→
    31→open Finset Fintype
    32→
    33→namespace SimpleGraph
    34→
    35→section IsExtremal
    36→
    37→variable {V : Type*} [Fintype V] {G : SimpleGraph V} [DecidableRel G.Adj]
    38→
    39→/-- `G` is an extremal graph satisfying `p` if `G` has the maximum number of edges of any simple
    40→graph, with fixed vertices, satisfying `p`. -/
    41→def IsExtremal (G : SimpleGraph V) [DecidableRel G.Adj] (p : SimpleGraph V → Prop) :=
    42→  p G ∧ ∀ ⦃G' : SimpleGraph V⦄ [DecidableRel G'.Adj], p G' → #G'.edgeFinset ≤ #G.edgeFinset
    43→
    44→lemma IsExtremal.prop {p : SimpleGraph V → Prop} (h : G.IsExtremal p) : p G := h.1
    45→
    46→open Classical in
    47→/-- If one simple graph satisfies `p`, then there exists an extremal graph satisfying `p`. -/
    48→theorem exists_isExtremal_iff_exists (p : SimpleGraph V → Prop) :
    49→    (∃ G : SimpleGraph V, ∃ _ : DecidableRel G.Adj, G.IsExtremal p) ↔ ∃ G, p G := by
    50→  refine ⟨fun ⟨_, _, h⟩ ↦ ⟨_, h.1⟩, fun ⟨G, hp⟩ ↦ ?_⟩
    51→  obtain ⟨G', hp', h⟩ := by
    52→    apply exists_max_image { G | p G } (#·.edgeFinset)
    53→    use G, by simpa using hp
    54→  use G', inferInstanceAs (DecidableRel G'.Adj)
    55→  exact ⟨by simpa using hp', fun _ _ hp ↦ by convert h _ (by simpa using hp)⟩
    56→
    57→/-- If `H` has at least one edge, then there exists an extremal `H.Free` graph. -/
    58→theorem exists_isExtremal_free {W : Type*} {H : SimpleGraph W} (h : H ≠ ⊥) :
    59→    ∃ G : SimpleGraph V, ∃ _ : DecidableRel G.Adj, G.IsExtremal H.Free :=
    60→  (exists_isExtremal_iff_exists H.Free).mpr ⟨⊥, free_bot h⟩
    61→
    62→open Classical in
    63→theorem IsExtremal.le_iff_eq
    64→    {p : SimpleGraph V → Prop} (hG : G.IsExtremal p) {H : SimpleGraph V} (hH : p H) :
    65→    G ≤ H ↔ G = H :=
    66→  ⟨fun hGH ↦ edgeFinset_inj.1 <|
    67→    eq_of_subset_of_card_le (edgeFinset_subset_edgeFinset.2 hGH) (hG.2 hH), le_of_eq⟩
    68→
    69→end IsExtremal
    70→
    71→section ExtremalNumber
    72→
    73→open Classical in
    74→/-- The extremal number of a natural number `n` and a simple graph `H` is the maximum number of
    75→edges in a `H`-free simple graph on `n` vertices.
    76→
    77→If `H` is contained in all simple graphs on `n` vertices, then this is `0`. -/
    78→noncomputable def extremalNumber (n : ℕ) {W : Type*} (H : SimpleGraph W) : ℕ :=
    79→  sup { G : SimpleGraph (Fin n) | H.Free G } (#·.edgeFinset)
    80→
    81→variable {n : ℕ} {V W : Type*} {G : SimpleGraph V} {H : SimpleGraph W}
    82→
    83→open Classical in
    84→theorem extremalNumber_of_fintypeCard_eq [Fintype V] (hc : card V = n) :
    85→    extremalNumber n H = sup { G : SimpleGraph V | H.Free G } (#·.edgeFinset) := by
    86→  let e := Fintype.equivFinOfCardEq hc
    87→  rw [extremalNumber, le_antisymm_iff]
    88→  and_intros
    89→  on_goal 1 =>
    90→    replace e := e.symm
    91→  all_goals
    92→  rw [Finset.sup_le_iff]
    93→  intro G h
    94→  let G' := G.map e.toEmbedding
    95→  replace h' : G' ∈ univ.filter (H.Free ·) := by
    96→    rw [mem_filter, ← free_congr .refl (.map e G)]
    97→    simpa using h
    98→  rw [Iso.card_edgeFinset_eq (.map e G)]
    99→  convert @le_sup _ _ _ _ { G | H.Free G } (#·.edgeFinset) G' h'
   100→
   101→variable [Fintype V] [DecidableRel G.Adj]
   102→
   103→/-- If `G` is `H`-free, then `G` has at most `extremalNumber (card V) H` edges. -/
   104→theorem card_edgeFinset_le_extremalNumber (h : H.Free G) :
   105→    #G.edgeFinset ≤ extremalNumber (card V) H := by
   106→  rw [extremalNumber_of_fintypeCard_eq rfl]
   107→  convert @le_sup _ _ _ _ { G | H.Free G } (#·.edgeFinset) G (by simpa using h)
   108→
   109→/-- If `G` has more than `extremalNumber (card V) H` edges, then `G` contains a copy of `H`. -/
   110→theorem IsContained.of_extremalNumber_lt_card_edgeFinset
   111→    (h : extremalNumber (card V) H < #G.edgeFinset) : H ⊑ G := by
   112→  contrapose h; push_neg
   113→  exact card_edgeFinset_le_extremalNumber h
   114→
   115→/-- `extremalNumber (card V) H` is at most `x` if and only if every `H`-free simple graph `G` has
   116→at most `x` edges. -/
   117→theorem extremalNumber_le_iff (H : SimpleGraph W) (m : ℕ) :
   118→    extremalNumber (card V) H ≤ m ↔
   119→      ∀ ⦃G : SimpleGraph V⦄ [DecidableRel G.Adj], H.Free G → #G.edgeFinset ≤ m := by
   120→  simp_rw [extremalNumber_of_fintypeCard_eq rfl, Finset.sup_le_iff, mem_filter_univ]
   121→  exact ⟨fun h _ _ h' ↦ by convert h _ h', fun h _ h' ↦ by convert h h'⟩
   122→
   123→/-- `extremalNumber (card V) H` is greater than `x` if and only if there exists a `H`-free simple
   124→graph `G` with more than `x` edges. -/
   125→theorem lt_extremalNumber_iff (H : SimpleGraph W) (m : ℕ) :
   126→    m < extremalNumber (card V) H ↔
   127→      ∃ G : SimpleGraph V, ∃ _ : DecidableRel G.Adj, H.Free G ∧ m < #G.edgeFinset := by
   128→  simp_rw [extremalNumber_of_fintypeCard_eq rfl, Finset.lt_sup_iff, mem_filter_univ]
   129→  exact ⟨fun ⟨_, h, h'⟩ ↦ ⟨_, _, h, h'⟩, fun ⟨_, _, h, h'⟩ ↦ ⟨_, h, by convert h'⟩⟩
   130→
   131→variable {R : Type*} [Semiring R] [LinearOrder R] [FloorSemiring R]
   132→
   133→@[inherit_doc extremalNumber_le_iff]
   134→theorem extremalNumber_le_iff_of_nonneg (H : SimpleGraph W) {m : R} (h : 0 ≤ m) :
   135→    extremalNumber (card V) H ≤ m ↔
   136→      ∀ ⦃G : SimpleGraph V⦄ [DecidableRel G.Adj], H.Free G → #G.edgeFinset ≤ m := by
   137→  simp_rw [← Nat.le_floor_iff h]
   138→  exact extremalNumber_le_iff H ⌊m⌋₊
   139→
   140→@[inherit_doc lt_extremalNumber_iff]
   141→theorem lt_extremalNumber_iff_of_nonneg (H : SimpleGraph W) {m : R} (h : 0 ≤ m) :
   142→    m < extremalNumber (card V) H ↔
   143→      ∃ G : SimpleGraph V, ∃ _ : DecidableRel G.Adj, H.Free G ∧ m < #G.edgeFinset := by
   144→  simp_rw [← Nat.floor_lt h]
   145→  exact lt_extremalNumber_iff H ⌊m⌋₊
   146→
   147→/-- If `H` contains a copy of `H'`, then `extremalNumber n H` is at most `extremalNumber n H`. -/
   148→theorem IsContained.extremalNumber_le {W' : Type*} {H' : SimpleGraph W'} (h : H' ⊑ H) :
   149→    extremalNumber n H' ≤ extremalNumber n H := by
   150→  rw [← Fintype.card_fin n, extremalNumber_le_iff]
   151→  intro _ _ h'
   152→  contrapose! h'
   153→  exact h.trans (IsContained.of_extremalNumber_lt_card_edgeFinset h')
   154→
   155→/-- If `H₁ ≃g H₂`, then `extremalNumber n H₁` equals `extremalNumber n H₂`. -/
   156→@[congr]
   157→theorem extremalNumber_congr {n₁ n₂ : ℕ} {W₁ W₂ : Type*} {H₁ : SimpleGraph W₁}
   158→    {H₂ : SimpleGraph W₂} (h : n₁ = n₂) (e : H₁ ≃g H₂) :
   159→    extremalNumber n₁ H₁ = extremalNumber n₂ H₂ := by
   160→  rw [h, le_antisymm_iff]
   161→  and_intros
   162→  on_goal 2 =>
   163→    replace e := e.symm
   164→  all_goals
   165→    rw [← Fintype.card_fin n₂, extremalNumber_le_iff]
   166→    intro G _ h
   167→    apply card_edgeFinset_le_extremalNumber
   168→    contrapose! h
   169→    exact h.trans' ⟨e.toCopy⟩
   170→
   171→/-- If `H₁ ≃g H₂`, then `extremalNumber n H₁` equals `extremalNumber n H₂`. -/
   172→theorem extremalNumber_congr_right {W₁ W₂ : Type*} {H₁ : SimpleGraph W₁} {H₂ : SimpleGraph W₂}
   173→    (e : H₁ ≃g H₂) : extremalNumber n H₁ = extremalNumber n H₂ := extremalNumber_congr rfl e
   174→
   175→/-- `H`-free extremal graphs are `H`-free simple graphs having `extremalNumber (card V) H` many
   176→edges. -/
   177→theorem isExtremal_free_iff :
   178→    G.IsExtremal H.Free ↔ H.Free G ∧ #G.edgeFinset = extremalNumber (card V) H := by
   179→  rw [IsExtremal, and_congr_right_iff, ← extremalNumber_le_iff]
   180→  exact fun h ↦ ⟨eq_of_le_of_ge (card_edgeFinset_le_extremalNumber h), ge_of_eq⟩
   181→
   182→lemma card_edgeFinset_of_isExtremal_free (h : G.IsExtremal H.Free) :
   183→    #G.edgeFinset = extremalNumber (card V) H := (isExtremal_free_iff.mp h).2
   184→
   185→/-- If `G` is `H.Free`, then `G.deleteIncidenceSet v` is also `H.Free` and has at most
   186→`extremalNumber (card V-1) H` many edges. -/
   187→theorem card_edgeFinset_deleteIncidenceSet_le_extremalNumber
   188→    [DecidableEq V] (h : H.Free G) (v : V) :
   189→    #(G.deleteIncidenceSet v).edgeFinset ≤ extremalNumber (card V - 1) H := by
   190→  rw [← card_edgeFinset_induce_compl_singleton, ← @card_unique ({v} : Set V), ← card_compl_set]
   191→  apply card_edgeFinset_le_extremalNumber
   192→  contrapose! h
   193→  exact h.trans ⟨Copy.induce G {v}ᶜ⟩
   194→
   195→end ExtremalNumber
   196→
   197→end SimpleGraph
   198→
